clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guide acute blood pressure lowering in spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage to improve functional outcomes and reduce adverse events
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adult patients with acute spontaneous ICH, at presentation and during first 7 days
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: BP target selection (130-140 mm Hg) + titration strategy + safety threshold

navigationPath.purpose: Capture admission SBP and ICH diagnosis confirmation
Route based on SBP: 150-220, >220, or other categories
Guide BP medication titration and monitoring
Monitor for peaks and large variability in SBP
Check if SBP <130 mm Hg to stop antihypertensives
Continue monitoring for at least 7 days post-ICH

validationRules: Confirm spontaneous ICH before applying recommendations

rules: If SBP 150-220 mm Hg → apply COR 2a LOE A: lower SBP to 130-140 mm Hg for ≥7 days
If SBP 150-220 mm Hg and SBP drops <130 mm Hg → stop antihypertensive medications
If acute BP lowering required → apply COR 2a LOE B-NR: ensure smooth, nonlabile, sustained control avoiding peaks and large variability
If SBP >220 mm Hg → apply COR 3 Harm LOE B-NR: do not lower SBP below 130 mm Hg
Monitor functional outcomes (mRS, Barthel Index) at follow-up

categories.name: Moderate SBP Elevation
categories.action: Lower SBP to 130-140 mm Hg for ≥7 days
categories.stop_condition: If SBP <130 mm Hg, stop antihypertensives

categories.name: BP Lowering Strategy
categories.action: Smooth, nonlabile, sustained control, avoid peaks and large SBP variability

categories.name: Severe SBP Elevation
categories.action: Do not lower SBP below 130 mm Hg
categories.warning: Reducing below 130 mm Hg increases adverse events

bpTarget.description: e.g., 'SBP 130-140 mm Hg' or 'Do not lower below 130 mm Hg'

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Emergency neurosurgery consultation, consider intensive care admission
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Continue intensive BP monitoring, avoid lowering below 130 mm Hg

appDisplay.screenType: High severity alert with harm recommendation
appDisplay.button: Alert: Do not lower SBP below 130 mm Hg
appDisplay.behavior: Flag adverse event risk, escalate to ICU protocol

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Follow AHA/ASA guidelines for ICH management, neurology consultation
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor for at least 7 days post-ICH, assess functional outcomes at 30, 90 days
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Check SBP every 15-30 minutes during acute lowering, then hourly
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: If SBP >220 mm Hg or <130 mm Hg with medications → alert attending physician
nextStepRouting.documentation: Record BP targets achieved, medication doses, SBP variability metrics, functional outcome scores
